component.name: KycVerificationWizard
component.business_context: A 3-step Know-Your-Customer (KYC) identity verification wizard: personal information, document upload, and selfie capture/upload, ending in a verification status outcome (pending review / approved / rejected).

props.name: personalInfo
props.type: { fields: Array<{ name: string; label: string; value: string; error?: string; hint?: string; required?: boolean }> }

props.name: document
props.type: { status: 'empty' | 'drag-active' | 'selected' | 'loading' | 'error' | 'success'; file?: { name: string; size: number; type: string }; error?: string }

states.name: step-personal-info
states.a11y: Step region has a heading (e.g. 'Step 1 of 3: Personal information') that receives focus on entry via tabIndex={-1} + focus(); wizard root uses role="form" with aria-labelledby pointing to that heading.

states.name: step-document-upload
states.a11y: Step heading ('Step 2 of 3: Document upload') receives focus on entry; dropzone is described by aria-describedby listing accepted types and size limit.

states.name: step-selfie
states.a11y: Step heading ('Step 3 of 3: Selfie verification') receives focus on entry; verification status panel (if present) is rendered below the capture control.

states.name: step-indicator-current
states.a11y: aria-current="step" on the active segment; visually hidden text: '{Label}, current step'. State conveyed by filled background + bold text + aria-current, not color alone.

states.name: step-indicator-completed
states.a11y: Checkmark Icon (aria-hidden) plus visually hidden text: '{Label}, completed'. Success color meets ≥3:1 against background.default; icon+text are the non-color signal (WCAG 1.4.1).

states.name: step-indicator-upcoming
states.a11y: Uses color.border.strong (not color.border.default, which fails 3:1) for the segment outline, plus the step number and visually hidden text: '{Label}, upcoming'. Non-color signal: unfilled outline + number + text.

states.name: back-navigation
states.a11y: Rendered as Button variant="secondary"; disabled prop set (not aria-disabled) when isBackDisabled is true so it is removed from the tab order's operable state but still announced as disabled.

states.name: next-navigation
states.trigger: always rendered; disabled via isNextDisabled, busy via isNextLoading
states.a11y: Rendered as Button variant="primary"; disabled when isNextDisabled; when isNextLoading, Button loading prop shows an inline Spinner with aria-label="Validating…" inside a role="status" live region so AT users aren't left waiting silently.

states.name: upload-empty
states.a11y: Dropzone is a labelled, keyboard-focusable button (tabIndex 0) wrapping a visually-hidden native <input type="file">; Enter/Space opens the file picker. aria-label: 'Drag and drop or browse. PDF, JPG, PNG. Max 10MB.'

states.name: upload-drag-active
states.a11y: Border color changes to color.action.primary.default (visual-only affordance is supplemented by a visually hidden live region announcing 'Drop to upload' via role="status") so screen reader users are not left without feedback during drag.

states.name: upload-file-selected
states.a11y: File name/size rendered as text (not color-only); a labelled 'Remove file' Icon button (accessible name: 'Remove {fileName}') is keyboard operable and returns focus to the dropzone after removal.

states.name: verification-pending-review
states.a11y: Rendered via Alert tone="warning" role="status" (polite, non-blocking outcome); icon + text convey state, not color alone.

states.name: verification-approved
states.a11y: Rendered via Alert tone="success" role="status"; icon + text convey state, not color alone.

states.name: verification-rejected
states.a11y: Rendered via Alert tone="error" role="alert" (assertive, requires user action); icon + text convey state, not color alone.

states.name: loading
states.a11y: A Spinner with an explicit aria-label ('Uploading document…' / 'Verifying identity…' / 'Validating…') is rendered inside a role="status" (aria-live="polite") live region; the corresponding control (dropzone, capture button, or Next) is disabled for the duration.

states.name: error
states.trigger: personalInfo field has .error, or document.status === 'error', or selfie.status === 'error'
states.a11y: Field-level errors: FormField error prop + aria-invalid="true" + aria-describedby on the Input. Upload/selfie errors: Alert tone="error" role="alert" bound to the dropzone/capture control via aria-describedby, distinguishing 'unsupported file type' vs 'file exceeds 10MB' with distinct messages.

states.name: disabled
states.trigger: disabled === true (whole wizard), or isBackDisabled / isNextDisabled per-control
states.a11y: color.text.disabled and color.action.primary.disabled are confined strictly to disabled control text/backgrounds (never used for readable body/status copy); disabled state set via the native disabled attribute on Button/Input, not visual styling alone.

states.name: focus
states.trigger: any interactive element (step segment, dropzone, file-remove button, Back, Next, form inputs) receives keyboard focus
states.a11y: A ≥2px focus-visible outline using color.focus.ring (≥3:1 contrast) is applied via :focus-visible to step indicator segments, the dropzone, the file-remove control, Back/Next buttons, and all form fields.

states.name: empty
states.trigger: personalInfo.fields all pristine/unfilled (step-personal-info), or document.status === 'empty', or selfie.status === 'empty'
states.a11y: Pristine empty state is visually and semantically distinct from a filled-but-invalid state: no aria-invalid is set until the user has interacted with or attempted to submit the field (avoids premature error announcements).

states.name: success
states.trigger: document.status === 'success' (document accepted, distinct from verification-approved) or a personal-info field passes validation on blur
states.a11y: Step-level success acknowledgment (e.g. a success Icon next to the uploaded file, or a completed step-indicator segment) is announced via a visually hidden 'Document uploaded successfully' string inside a role="status" region; kept distinct from the terminal verification-approved state.

responsive.behavior: Below breakpoint.sm, the step indicator collapses from three labeled inline segments to a compact numeric form ('Step 2 of 3: Document upload') using font.size.sm; the document-upload dropzone becomes a full-width tap target sized for the file-picker button (drag-and-drop affordance is retained but de-emphasized since it is not viable on touch).

Below breakpoint.md, personal-info form fields stack vertically (single column, space.4 gap) instead of any multi-column layout; Back/Next controls stack full-width and reorder so Next (primary action) is reachable by thumb (rendered first/bottom) without horizontal scrolling.
At breakpoint.lg and above, personal-info fields may render in a 2-column grid (space.6 gutter) inside a Card with padding="lg"; Back and Next render side by side, Next right-aligned.

accessibility: Wizard root uses role="form" with aria-labelledby referencing the current step's heading; on every Back/Next transition, focus programmatically moves to the new step's heading (tabIndex={-1} + .focus()) so keyboard/AT users are never stranded on a removed button
Step indicator is a list (ol/li) with aria-current="step" on the active item and visually hidden text conveying '{label}, current|completed|upcoming' for every segment — state is never color-only

Completed step-indicator segments use color.feedback.success (≥3:1) plus a checkmark Icon; upcoming segments use color.border.strong (not color.border.default, which fails the 3:1 UI-component contrast minimum) plus the step number as a non-color signal

Document upload dropzone is a labelled, keyboard-operable control (native <input type="file"> triggered via a real button/label, not a bare div onClick) reachable by Tab and activatable with Enter/Space; aria-describedby states accepted types (PDF, JPG, PNG) and the 10MB limit
Drag-active state is announced to screen reader users via a role="status" live region ('Drop to upload'), not conveyed by border color alone
File type/size validation errors (wrong type, >10MB) are surfaced via Alert role="alert" bound to the dropzone through aria-describedby and aria-invalid, with distinct copy per error cause
Selected file has a keyboard-focusable, labelled 'Remove {fileName}' control implemented with the Icon component plus an accessible name — never a decorative icon alone

Verification status uses role="status" (Alert tone="warning"/"success") for pending-review and approved outcomes, and role="alert" (Alert tone="error") for rejected, since rejection requires user action

All async operations (document upload processing, verification determination, Next-button validation) use a Spinner with an explicit aria-label describing what is loading, wrapped in a role="status" (aria-live="polite") region

Every personal-info field is bound through FormField (label + htmlFor) with aria-describedby for hint/error text and aria-invalid="true" set only after interaction/attempted submission, not on initial pristine render
A visible ≥2px focus ring using color.focus.ring is applied via :focus-visible to the step indicator segments, dropzone, file-remove control, Back/Next buttons, and every form field
Step-transition, upload-progress, and spinner animations are wrapped in a prefers-reduced-motion media query and disabled/shortened when the OS preference is set

color.text.disabled and color.action.primary.disabled are confined strictly to genuinely disabled control text/backgrounds and are always paired with the native disabled attribute — never used as the sole cue for state or for any readable body/status text
open_questions: Personal-info fields are not enumerated in the brief (no names, DOB, address, national ID, etc. specified); the props model exposes a generic `personalInfo.fields[]` array rather than named fields — the concrete field set must be supplied by the caller/host application before this can be considered field-complete.

The brief does not specify whether the selfie step uses live device-camera capture, file upload, or both; the `selfie` prop models a generic captured-image/status shape (`selfie.status`, `selfie.imageUrl`) that supports either implementation, but camera-permission-denied / no-camera-detected handling is not modeled as a distinct enum value pending clarification.

No design-system token exists for a 'subtle' success or warning background (only color.feedback.error.subtle is defined) to safely pair with color.feedback.success / color.feedback.warning as a tinted status-card background; the spec falls back to color.background.subtle (a neutral surface) for the pending-review and approved Alert backgrounds instead of inventing new success/warning-subtle tokens.

Whether the transition from 'pending review' to 'approved'/'rejected' is synchronous (immediately after selfie submission) or asynchronous/polled is not specified; the `verification` prop is modeled as caller-supplied state (not internally polled) so the host application owns that timing decision.

Whether a document upload allows exactly one file or multiple is not specified; the spec assumes single-document upload per the brief's singular phrasing ('document upload'), reflected in `document` being a single object rather than an array — flagged as an assumption carried over from extraction.

No token pair is specified for the upcoming step-indicator marker's fill/outline against color.background.default beyond color.border.strong (~2.5:1, below the 3:1 UI-component minimum in isolation); the spec mitigates this by requiring the step number + text as the primary non-color signal rather than relying on the outline's contrast alone, since no stronger neutral border token exists in tokens.json.
